original_description: Generate user-facing changelogs from git history. Transforms technical commits into clear, customer-friendly release notes. Categorizes changes, filters noise, and formats professionally. Triggers: "create changelog", "release notes", "what changed since", "generate changelog", "update CHANGELOG.md", or as an optional step in finishing-a-development-branch when the branch contains user-facing changes.
best_description: Generate user-facing changelogs from git history. Transforms technical commits into clear, customer-friendly release notes. Categorizes changes, filters noise, and formats professionally. Triggers: "create changelog", "release notes", "what changed since", "generate changelog", "update CHANGELOG.md", or as an optional step in finishing-a-development-branch when the branch contains user-facing changes.
final_description: Use this skill when the user wants to publish information about product changes to an external or non-technical audience — transforming git commits into polished release notes, changelogs, or update summaries. The audience signal is decisive: end users, customers, PMs, app store reviewers, or anyone outside the engineering team. Do NOT trigger when the documentation is for internal technical consumers (DBAs, developers, architects) or when the goal is understanding/analyzing git history rather than publishing it.

history.description: Generate user-facing changelogs from git history. Transforms technical commits into clear, customer-friendly release notes. Categorizes changes, filters noise, and formats professionally. Triggers: "create changelog", "release notes", "what changed since", "generate changelog", "update CHANGELOG.md", or as an optional step in finishing-a-development-branch when the branch contains user-facing changes.

train_results.query: write up the release notes for this branch before i merge it
our PM wants a changelog for the mobile team, can you pull from git commits
changlelog for last 2 weeks of commits plz
create a changelog for the v2.3.0 release
what shipped in this release? summarize git log into something non-technical for stakeholders
generate release notes from git history since last tag
what commits broke the tests? trace the regression in git history
document what changed in the database schema for the DBA team
summarize the git history of the auth module so i can understand how it evolved
what changed in this file since last week
git log shows a lot of noise, help me understand what this branch actually does
write up a postmortem on the outage, include what changed before it happened

test_results.query: transform these commits into user-facing release notes, ignore the chore and ci stuff
what changed since v1.5.0? need something customer-friendly
update CHANGELOG.md with everything merged this sprint
need to update docs/CHANGELOG.md before cutting the 3.0 release
i'm finishing up this feature branch, review it before i open a PR
create a migration guide for users upgrading from v1 to v2
generate a summary of all commits by the backend team this quarter
write release notes for this API endpoint explaining how to use it

history.description: Use this skill when the user wants to produce user-facing release notes or changelogs from git commits — transforming technical commit history into polished, customer-readable summaries of what shipped. Covers version releases, sprint summaries, app store updates, and PR merge notes. The key signal is the *audience*: end users, customers, PMs, or non-technical stakeholders reading about product changes. Do NOT trigger for internal technical documentation (schema docs, architecture notes, DBA reports), git forensics (tracing regressions, understanding file history), or developer-oriented analysis of what a branch does.
